message:
fix(pane): coalesce resize history by gesture

Closes bd-1wg6i

 {"id":"bd-1wevm.3","title":"task(succinct): Benchmark succinct vs dense at various list sizes","description":"## Summary

Quantitative comparison of succinct data structures (Elias-Fano, LOUDS) vs dense arrays/pointer trees across a range of list sizes to identify the crossover point.

## Detail

Benchmark matrix:
- List sizes: 100, 1K, 10K, 100K, 1M rows
- Operations: random access, range query, sequential scan, insert (rebuild)
- Data structures: dense array, Elias-Fano, LOUDS (for tree), pointer tree
- Metrics: memory usage, query latency (p50/p95/p99), build time

Expected crossover points:
- Elias-Fano beats dense for memory at ~10K rows
- Elias-Fano beats dense for cache performance at ~100K rows (fits in L2 vs spills to L3)
- LOUDS beats pointer tree at ~50K nodes for memory
- Dense may beat succinct for query latency at <10K rows (simpler code, fewer cache misses)

The crossover analysis drives the automatic selection: use dense below threshold, succinct above.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Full benchmark matrix with memory and latency measurements
2. Clear identification of crossover points for each operation
3. Automatic fallback threshold derived from benchmarks
4. Results stored as CI artifacts
5. Recommendation: which data structure to use at each scale","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:35.721453552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-05T21:24:27.114609634Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T21:24:27.114584707Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","graveyard","succinct"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1wevm.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1wevm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1wevm.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1wevm.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1wevm.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1wevm.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1wevm.4","title":"task(succinct): Property tests — succinct query results match dense","description":"## Summary

Property-based testing to verify that succinct data structures (Elias-Fano, LOUDS) produce identical query results to their dense counterparts for all operations.

## Detail

Property tests using proptest:

1. **Elias-Fano vs dense array:**
   - Generate random sorted sequences of length 1..10K
   - For each sequence, verify: access(i) matches dense[i] for all i
   - For each sequence, verify: rank(v) matches linear scan count for random v
   - For each sequence, verify: select(r) matches dense scan for random r
   - For each sequence, verify: next_geq(v) matches linear scan for random v

2. **LOUDS vs pointer tree:**
   - Generate random trees with 1..1K nodes and branching factor 1..10
   - For each tree, verify: parent(v) matches pointer-based parent for all v
   - For each tree, verify: first_child(v) matches pointer-based for all v
   - For each tree, verify: next_sibling(v) matches pointer-based for all v
   - For each tree, verify: is_leaf(v) matches pointer-based for all v
   - For each tree, verify: DFS traversal order is identical

3. **Round-trip tests:**
   - Build succinct from dense, query all elements, verify identity
   - Rebuild succinct from same data, verify bit-identical encoding

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All property tests pass with 10K+ cases each
2. Shrinking produces minimal failing cases when tests fail
3. Edge cases covered: empty sequence, single element, maximum size, all-same values
4. Tests run in CI with reproducible seeds","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:46.302695368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-05T20:45:01.786760026Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T20:45:01.786739979Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["graveyard","property-test","succinct"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1wevm.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1wevm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1wevm.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1wevm.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1wevm.5","title":"task(succinct): E2E integration tests with structured JSONL logging","description":"## Summary

End-to-end integration tests for succinct data structures (Elias-Fano, LOUDS) verifying correct behavior within the virtualized list/tree widget pipeline, with structured JSONL evidence logging.

## Background

Succinct data structures operate at the bit level. Off-by-one errors in rank/select are silent and catastrophic — they cause the wrong row to render at the wrong pixel offset. E2E tests must verify the full pipeline from user scrolls to offset Y to correct row is visible.

## Detail

### E2E Tests

1. **Scroll-to-offset**: Build 100K-row list with Elias-Fano height sums, scroll to random offsets, verify correct visible rows
2. **Viewport resize**: Resize viewport during scroll, verify visible rows update correctly with succinct backing
3. **Dynamic insert/delete**: Insert/remove rows, rebuild succinct structure, verify scroll position preserved
4. **Threshold crossover**: Test at 99K rows (dense) and 101K rows (succinct), verify identical visible output
5. **LOUDS tree navigation**: Build 50K-node tree widget with LOUDS backing, verify expand/collapse/scroll
6. **Round-trip**: Dense -> succinct -> query all -> compare with dense queries, verify bitwise identical
7. **Degenerate inputs**: All same heights, zero heights, maximum heights, single row

### Structured Logging

```json
{"test":"scroll_to_offset","row_count":100000,"scroll_offset_px":452300,"expected_first_row":12045,"actual_first_row":12045,"query_ns":42,"encoding":"elias_fano"}
{"test":"threshold_crossover","row_count":101000,"encoding":"elias_fano","memory_bytes":31200,"dense_memory_bytes":808000,"ratio":0.039}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. 7+ E2E tests covering scroll, resize, dynamic mutation, threshold, tree, round-trip, and degenerate inputs
2. All tests emit structured JSONL with row counts, memory usage, query latency, and correctness status
3. Memory comparison report: succinct vs dense at each test size
4. Performance report: query latency percentiles at each test size
5. CI artifacts: JSONL logs, memory report, latency report
6. Zero tolerance: any incorrect visible row is a blocking failure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:23:18.370936521Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-05T21:30:26.452103816Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T21:30:26.452082006Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","graveyard","logging","succinct","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1wevm.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1wevm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1wevm.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1wevm.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1wevm.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1wevm.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1x6yx","title":"Render: buffer output + viewport transform","description":"Goal
- Render layout into Buffer with viewport transforms AND optional viewport size overrides.

Deliverables
- Correct scissoring/viewport sizing for 80x24, 120x40, 200x60.
- Respect size override (w/h) when set.
- Render output deterministic for fixed state.

Acceptance
- Snapshots stable across runs; viewport changes update output.

 {"id":"bd-1xhje","title":"Inline present cleanup on I/O error","description":"TerminalWriter::present_inline uses ? on writes; on IO error it returns before restoring cursor/sync/scroll-region, leaving the terminal in a bad state if the runtime keeps going. Add best-effort cleanup on error.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"TurquoiseBeacon","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:49:38.633291975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:06:18.594195276Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:06:18.594177763Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: present_inline wraps errors and calls best_effort_inline_cleanup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
